rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical three-component vicinal sulfenyltetrazolation of unactivated alkenes using thiols, TMSN3, and acetonitrile via a Ritter-type intermediate that enables 1,3-dipole cycloaddition.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of triarylamine mediator (Ar3N) to its radical cation, which performs SET on thiol to generate a thiyl radical; cathode reduces protons to H2.
electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Ar3N to Ar3N•+, which oxidizes/deprotonates thiol to thiyl radical; thiyl adds to alkene giving a C-radical that is further oxidized to an episulfonium cation, intercepted by MeCN to form a Ritter-type nitrilium that undergoes [3+2] with azide from TMSN3 to give the tetrazole; cathode reduces H+ to H2.

probe_reagents.name: disulfide (RSSR, 30)
probe_reagents.observation: Use of disulfide instead of thiol also gave vicinal sulfenyltetrazole 4 in comparable yield, supporting involvement of RS•/RSSR equilibrium.

probe_reagents.name: BHT (2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-methylphenol)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction suppressed (n.d.); BHT-SR adduct detected by GC-MS, indicating thiyl radical involvement.

probe_reagents.name: 1-(2-phenylcyclopropyl)-vinyl)benzene (31)
probe_reagents.observation: Afforded ring-opened product 32 in 20% yield (7.6:1 dr), consistent with a carbon-centered radical intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of Ar3N
probe_reagents.observation: Catalytic current and disappearance of Ar3N•+ reduction peak upon adding thiol; further enhanced with alkene, implicating Ar3N in two oxidation steps.

notes: Two pathways to the episulfonium ion (C) are proposed: path a via oxidation of β-thio C-radical (B); path b via reaction of alkene with bis(arylthio)sulfonium ion (G) derived from disulfide (F). Competing nucleophilic addition of TMSN3 to C would give the off-pathway vicinal azidosulfide (H). Phosphate additive (NaPF6/Bu4NClO4 with phosphate) prolongs Ar3N•+ lifetime.
